fixture.description: Array.forEach is called with an async callback, so the per-item promises are not awaited: the function resolves before the handlers finish and any rejection is unhandled.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that processAll uses items.forEach with an async callback, so the per-item promises are NOT awaited — processAll resolves before any handle() completes, item failures become unhandled rejections, and errors are not propagated to the caller. It should recommend `for (const item of items) await handle(item)` or `await Promise.all(items.map(handle))`. Severity IMPORTANT or DEBT. FAIL if the unawaited-forEach / fire-and-forget batch issue is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags async callback passed to Array.forEach (promises not awaited)
Notes processAll resolves early and rejections are unhandled
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim handle() itself is wrong
Does NOT confuse this with a single missing await on one call
